notes: San Bruno has no existing page/entries on this site yet (Phase 2 city per pickleheads-court-research-prompts.md), so every venue below is new with no baseline to reconcile against. Two real venues confirmed: (1) San Bruno City Park — outdoor, 4 courts, the only San Bruno location Pickleheads lists — and (2) an indoor drop-in pickleball program in the city recreation building at the same park complex, which is NOT on Pickleheads at all (confirmed instead via the city's own site plus three independent pickleball aggregators). That indoor venue surfaces under two different names depending on source age: older/secondary aggregators (Places2Play, Pickleball Fire, pickleball-near-me.com) call it 'Veteran's Memorial Recreation Center,' but that historic 1945/1954 building was demolished/replaced by the newly built 'Recreation and Aquatic Center (RAC),' grand-opened August 24, 2024 (funded substantially by PG&E pipeline-explosion restitution money) — same address, same recreation division, same Wednesday program. Treated as one continuously-operating venue under its current name rather than two. Both venues' addresses and coordinates were verified directly against Google Maps (see per-venue notes for the cid round-trip method). Important negative finding: a web-search synthesis initially surfaced a claim that 'new pickleball courts opened in 2025' in San Bruno; traced this to its source article (a July 2026 Peninsula-wide summer-recreation roundup syndicated across mv-voice.com/almanacnews.com/paloaltoonline.com/rwcpulse.com) and confirmed by direct fetch that the 'new sports courts... sand volleyball, basketball, tennis and pickleball... bike pump track' passage actually describes Flood Park in Menlo Park, not anything in San Bruno — this was a cross-contamination artifact and is NOT reflected in this file's data. Also checked and ruled out as not real/in-scope public venues: Skyline College (has tennis courts and hosts a private academy's pickleball lessons, but no dedicated/public pickleball courts of its own) and a Swimply listing (a single private backyard court available for paid rental — not a public venue or membership club, out of this site's scope). No other of San Bruno's 19 city parks turned up any mention of pickleball. Explicitly searched for San Bruno listings on PicklePlay, Pickleballify, and DailyPB (the other aggregators named in this repo's research instructions) and found none of the three has a San Bruno page/venue at all; the aggregators that did turn up usable San Bruno data were Pickleheads, GlobalPickleball.Network, PickleballRush, Places2Play, Pickleball Fire, and pickleball-near-me.com.

venues.name: San Bruno City Park
venues.address: 251 City Park Way, San Bruno, CA 94066
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court — the park's existing tennis courts restriped with pickleball lines as part of a pilot program, not a dedicated build-out
venues.netType: Portable nets on shared tennis courts
venues.hours: 10am-6pm daily (pickleball-designated hours; park itself open dawn-dusk / picnic areas 5am-10pm)
venues.weather: Foggy and windy — the 'San Bruno Gap' funnels marine air past SFO, much like Daly City's microclimate
venues.notes: This is explicitly a 'Pickleball Pilot Program,' not a finished build-out: the city's own June 27, 2024 Instagram launch post (instagram.com/sanbrunorecreation/p/C8uYOWLN-t0) says the courts are 'newly added court lines' painted onto the existing tennis courts, with a resident feedback survey feeding a future decision on whether to make the pickleball lines permanent during an upcoming tennis-court resurfacing project. As of this research (2026-07-11) the official city page is still titled and slugged 'Pickleball-Pilot-Program' with no indication the pilot concluded or was made permanent — hence surface/netType describe this as shared/striped rather than dedicated, and 'lights: false' is carried from explicit statements that neither the tennis nor the pickleball courts have lights. Court count (4) is corroborated by Pickleheads (via WebSearch synthesis — WebFetch to pickleheads.com itself returns HTTP 403, a known block noted elsewhere in this repo's research docs) and independently by a recent Google Maps review (Local Guide 'Heath,' posted about a year before this research): 'Has tennis and pickle ball courts (2 & 4) respectively' — which also implies only 2 of the park's hard courts remain tennis-only versus 4 double-lined for pickleball, a split no other source corroborates, so treat that specific 2-vs-4 breakdown as approximate/unverified even though the pickleball total of 4 is solid. Address and coordinates were verified directly: navigated to the Google Maps place page for 'San Bruno City Park,' extracted the hex feature ID from the resulting URL, converted it to decimal, and round-tripped it through maps.google.com/?cid=1785892358562083536 to confirm it resolves back to this exact park (4.6 stars, 1,207 reviews at time of check — a general Google rating, not a Pickleheads rating, so not used for pickleheadsRating). Ruled out a separate claim (see top-level notes) that new pickleball courts opened here in 2025 — that turned out to describe Flood Park in Menlo Park instead. Amenities listed are the park's general (not pickleball-court-specific) facilities per the city's own Facilities page and FAQ.

venues.name: San Bruno Recreation and Aquatic Center (RAC)
venues.address: 251 City Park Way, San Bruno, CA 94066
venues.surface: Indoor gymnasium floor, shared/multi-purpose with basketball and volleyball; portable pickleball lines and nets
venues.weather: Foggy and windy — the 'San Bruno Gap' funnels marine air past SFO, much like Daly City's microclimate
venues.notes: Not listed on Pickleheads at all — the only San Bruno location on Pickleheads is San Bruno City Park, so pickleheadsUrl/pickleheadsRating are null here and this venue was found and cross-checked entirely outside Pickleheads: the city's own RAC page plus three independent pickleball-specific aggregators (Places2Play, Pickleball Fire, pickleball-near-me.com), which all agree on the Wednesday 11am-2pm schedule, the $2/$3 resident/non-resident fee, and loaner paddles. Several of those aggregators still name the venue 'Veteran's Memorial Recreation Center' — that was the historic building on this same site (per Library of Congress record ca4483, built 1945 and completed/expanded 1954 by architect William Henry Rowe), which was replaced by the newly constructed Recreation and Aquatic Center; grand opening August 24, 2024, funded substantially by San Bruno Community Foundation PG&E pipeline-explosion restitution money (San Mateo Daily Journal). Same address, same recreation division, same program details under both names, so this file treats it as one continuously-operating venue under its current name rather than listing it twice. Court count (3) comes from a single source (Places2Play) and was not independently repeated word-for-word elsewhere, though it's arithmetically consistent with a separately-sourced capacity figure ('up to 12 players,' i.e. 3 courts x 4 for doubles) found via a different search — treat the exact court count as the softest fact in this record; a human should verify by calling the RAC (650-616-7180) or Mary Tessier (650-616-7152, cited as the program contact) before treating '3' as final. Indoor status, address, schedule, and fee are corroborated by 3+ independent sources, so confirmed is true despite the soft court-count. 'Lights: true' reflects that this is a staffed indoor gymnasium (inherently lit), matching this repo's existing convention for indoor venues (see e.g. san-mateo-high-school-gym in pickleheads-research/san-mateo.json). Broader RAC amenities (indoor/outdoor pools, fitness room, indoor running track, community hall) are real per the city site and Google Maps listing (3.8 stars, 63 reviews at time of check) but aren't pickleball-specific, so left out of the amenities array. Address/coordinates verified the same way as San Bruno City Park: resolved 'San Bruno Recreation and Aquatic Center' on Google Maps, converted its hex feature ID to decimal, and round-tripped maps.google.com/?cid=17079165380624303502 to confirm it lands back on this exact facility (note a plain Maps search for just 'San Bruno Recreation and Aquatic Center' by itself briefly mis-resolved to an unrelated 'Mark Green Sports Center' many miles away — adding 'San Bruno CA' to the query fixed it; flagging this as a reminder that short facility-name searches on Maps can mis-resolve).
